title: War in Heaven
blurb: Sixty million years before mankind, the Necrons and their C'tan star-gods rose against the Old Ones and broke the galaxy's first order. The weapons of that war emptied whole star clusters, and some of them still sleep beneath dead worlds. Every empire since has merely fought in its ruins.
curatorNote: No M-scale stamp exists or can exist.

title: Birth of the Emperor
blurb: Legend places his birth in ancient Anatolia, eight thousand years before the first starship. One story claims a thousand human shamans died in a single pact to make him. For forty millennia he walks through human history unseen, steering it toward an empire only he can imagine.
curatorNote: Pre-M1 (~M-8). Off-scale.

title: Age of Terra
blurb: Fifteen millennia of slow, stubborn ascent: the first colony ships crawl out from Terra, and humanity learns to live under alien suns. No Imperium, no God-Emperor, just settlers and explorers writing mankind's first chapter. Almost nothing of it survives except the fact that it happened.
curatorNote: Span M1–M15 (the 'Golden Age').

title: Dark Age of Technology
blurb: Mankind's true golden age. STC machines build civilisation on demand, Men of Iron fight humanity's wars, and a thousand worlds flourish without a single prayer. Then the machines turn, the psykers wake, and the age ends so badly that all later history remembers paradise as darkness.

title: Age of Strife — Old Night
blurb: Warp storms slam shut around Terra and ten thousand colonies, and the interstellar age simply stops. Cut off, the worlds of men fall one by one to mutation, daemons and war. Five millennia of Old Night follow, long enough to forget there was ever light.

title: Fall of the Aeldari
blurb: The Aeldari empire dies in one night of its own making: millennia of cultivated excess birth Slaanesh, and the newborn god drinks the souls of a species. The death-scream tears open the Eye of Terror. Yet the same cataclysm calms the warp storms, and on distant Terra the Emperor's hour arrives.
curatorNote: Some HH-era fiction implies an earlier date; the community '750.M30' is soft. The storms abating after the Fall is the precondition for the Crusade (~798.M30).

title: Unification Wars
blurb: The Emperor steps out of Old Night's shadows and takes Terra back warlord by warlord, with the gene-forged Thunder Warriors as his hammer. The last battle is fought at Mount Ararat in 669. Victory secured, the Thunder Warriors are quietly purged; the Imperium learns early what it does to its own heroes.
curatorNote: Reaches back into late M29; ends well before the Crusade launches (798.M30).

title: Scattering of the Primarchs
blurb: The Chaos gods reach into the Emperor's gene-labs and hurl his twenty infant sons through the warp. They land on death worlds, feudal kingdoms and hive sumps across the galaxy, each raised by whatever world catches him. The Crusade to come is, among other things, a father collecting his stolen children.
curatorNote: No defensible canon year: Fandom's lone 792.M30 stamp (Perturabo's own estimate) contradicts primarch-derived proto-Legions fighting on Luna 703.M30. Curated shortly before Luna.

title: First Pacification of Luna
blurb: Three proto-Legions descend on the Selenar gene-cults and take Luna in six hours. It is the Imperium's first conquest beyond Terra and the first proof of what the Astartes are for. The gene-wrights of Luna are absorbed rather than destroyed; their craft will help build the Legions to come.
curatorNote: Both wikis' dedicated pages say 703.M30; a 'ca. 798' variant floats on some Legion pages — 703 is better attested.

title: Treaty of Mars
blurb: The Emperor offers Mars a bargain: the Mechanicum keeps its god and its mysteries, and in return arms the greatest war machine in human history. The priesthood signs. The double-headed Aquila becomes the seal of two empires fused into one, a marriage of convenience that outlasts every love.

title: Construction of the Astronomican
blurb: On Terra rises the Astronomican, a psychic lighthouse focused through a choir ten thousand strong and lit by the Emperor's own will. Without its beam, no ship can cross the warp beyond a handful of light years. The Crusade is launched on that light, and the Imperium never stops paying for it.
curatorNote: No canon year. Bracket: ordered after Unification (712), operational by the launch (798) — curated mid-bracket as a multi-decade megaproject.

title: Great Crusade
blurb: The storms lift and mankind's armies pour back into the stars. For two centuries the Legiones Astartes and their rediscovered primarchs bring a hundred thousand worlds into compliance, by diplomacy where possible and by annihilation where not. It is the most hopeful age the Imperium will ever know.

title: Horus Found on Cthonia
blurb: On the hive world Cthonia, almost within sight of Terra, the Emperor finds the first of his lost sons. Horus is brilliant, charming and hungry for his father's pride, and for decades he stands closest of all the brothers. No discovery will ever matter more, or cost more.
curatorNote: Discovery years per Fandom's Great Crusade Chronology; Lexicanum keeps only the order canonical (applies to all discovery rows).

title: Fulgrim Found on Chemos
blurb: On dying Chemos, Fulgrim has clawed a civilisation back from extinction through sheer perfectionism. He hands the Emperor his sword and pledges the Third Legion to the pursuit of perfection itself. The Phoenician will get his wish, and it will be the worst thing that ever happens to him.
curatorNote: GC-Chronology year.

title: Vulkan Found on Nocturne
blurb: Nocturne's volcanic trials breed hard people, and the blacksmith-king Vulkan is the hardest of them. Alone among the primarchs, he never stops counting the human cost of his wars. The XVIII Legion gains a father who values lives over glory, a rarity his brothers never quite understand.
curatorNote: GC-Chronology year.

title: Roboute Guilliman Found on Macragge
blurb: The Crusade reaches Macragge expecting another conquest and finds a functioning state instead. Roboute Guilliman has already built ordered, prosperous Ultramar with statecraft alone. The Emperor gives him the XIII Legion; Guilliman gives the Imperium the blueprint it will still be copying ten thousand years later.
curatorNote: 837.M30 also on the Guilliman page; Ultramar is seeded.

title: Rangdan Xenocides
blurb: In the galactic north the Crusade meets something it barely survives. The Rangda break Legions like no enemy before them; the Dark Angels bleed hardest, and whole formations vanish from the rolls. The victories cost so much that the records are sealed, and the Imperium chooses to forget its costliest war.
curatorNote: Lex: first battles c.839, great invasion 862, Third Xenocide 890s. First/Second boundary is source-split; the ~890 end is agreed.

title: Sanguinius Found on Baal
blurb: Among the rad-scarred tribes of Baal Secundus the Emperor finds a son with angel's wings, raised by mutants he refused to despise. Sanguinius becomes the best-loved of the brothers, the one to whom even the proud defer. His Legion inherits his nobility, and with it his hidden flaw.
curatorNote: Best-attested discovery date: 843.M30 on Fandom AND the official WarCom HH black-book extract.

title: Perturabo Found on Olympia
blurb: Perturabo of Olympia can calculate a siege to the hour and a slight to the day. The Emperor gives him the Iron Warriors, and the Crusade gives him every thankless trench war and meatgrinder fortress in its path. He keeps a perfect ledger of grievances, and one day he will collect.
curatorNote: GC-Chronology year.

title: Evacuation of Morningstar
blurb: Morningstar is dying, its storms tearing the planet apart, and two Legions race the apocalypse to carry its people off-world. Thousand Sons and Iron Warriors work side by side, Magnus and Perturabo briefly brothers in more than name. A small mercy in a violent age, remembered precisely because it stays rare.
curatorNote: Single source: Lexicanum's novel page.

title: Conquest of Galaspar
blurb: The Order of Galaspar rules its hives through starvation and terror, and the Death Guard are sent to break it. Mortarion's first campaign as a primarch is methodical, total and merciless. The Legion finds its signature here: no retreat, no fear of attrition, and very few survivors.
curatorNote: 'Mid-800s.M30', consistent with Mortarion taking command 854.

title: Lorgar Found on Colchis
blurb: Archpriest Lorgar has already won Colchis for the Emperor through holy war, years before ever meeting him. He greets his father as a god made flesh, and the Emperor lets the error stand far too long. The faith of Colchis will build perfect cities, and at Monarchia it will be answered with fire.

title: Jaghatai Khan Found on Chogoris
blurb: Jaghatai Khan has just finished conquering Chogoris when the Imperium arrives to tell him the galaxy is larger than his steppes. He takes the news as an invitation. The White Scars become the Crusade's swiftest blade: present everywhere, understood nowhere, and entirely content with that.

title: Dulan Campaign
blurb: On Dulan, Leman Russ and the Lion quarrel over the honour of a tyrant's death, and the argument becomes a duel lasting a day and a night. Russ eventually starts laughing; the Lion does not. Ten millennia later, Space Wolves and Dark Angels still ritually re-fight the grudge.

title: Konrad Curze Found on Nostramo
blurb: Nostramo never sees the sun, and the child who fell there learned to rule its murder-cities through fear alone. By the time the Emperor arrives, Konrad Curze has made crime itself afraid. He joins the Crusade haunted by visions of his own death, and never once doubts that they are true.
curatorNote: 896.M30, with Dorn, Fulgrim and Magnus present.

title: Angron Taken from Nuceria
blurb: Angron, gladiator-king of Nuceria, stands ready to die beside his slave rebellion when the Emperor simply teleports him away. His army is slaughtered without him. The Butcher's Nails keep grinding in his skull, and the War Hounds receive a father who never forgives his own rescue.

title: Razing of Monarchia
blurb: The Word Bearers conquer slowly because they raise cathedrals to the Emperor on every world. At Monarchia, the Emperor has the Ultramarines level their proudest city, then forces the entire Legion to kneel in its ashes. The lesson is meant to end worship. Instead it sends Lorgar looking for gods who want it.
curatorNote: Fandom: 964.M30 explicit; Lex derives '43 years before Isstvan V' (≈963) — kept 964, tension noted.

title: Compliance of Gardinaal
blurb: The Gardinaal answer compliance with defiance, and Ferrus Manus decides the Crusade has spent enough patience on them. The Iron Hands make an example so complete that other Legions formally protest. The Gorgon's lesson stands: the Imperium's mercy is a finite resource, and it is already running low.
curatorNote: No canon year. Bracket: 'as the Crusade draws to a close, a Warmaster to be chosen' — immediately pre-Ullanor, curated ~998.

title: Ullanor Crusade & Triumph
blurb: The greatest Ork empire in the galaxy is broken on Ullanor, and the Emperor stages the grandest triumph in human history on its ruins. He names Horus Warmaster, commander of the entire Crusade, then returns to Terra without explaining why. The brothers are left with glory, and with questions.
curatorNote: Sources split 000.M30/000.M31 — the Triumph reads as the M30→M31 hinge. Editorially the close of the Great Crusade chapter.

title: Council of Nikaea
blurb: On Nikaea the Emperor sits in judgement over psychic power, with Magnus himself speaking for sorcery. The verdict outlaws the Librarius and brands warpcraft a forbidden weapon. Magnus flies home certain his father is wrong. Of all the Heresy's causes, none is argued over more than this one.

title: Burning of Prospero
blurb: Magnus breaks the Edicts of Nikaea to warn Terra of Horus' coming betrayal, and the warning itself becomes his condemnation. Space Wolves, Custodes and Sisters of Silence burn Tizca, the city of light, to the ground. Magnus saves his sons by sorcery, straight into the Eye of Terror. Horus engineered every step of it.
curatorNote: A sanctioned reprisal under the Edicts of Nikaea; the buildup's flashpoint, just before Isstvan III.

title: Horus Heresy
blurb: The favoured son turns, and nine Legions turn with him. For a decade the Imperium the Crusade built tears itself apart, brother killing brother across a hundred thousand worlds. It is the wound from which everything else in this archive bleeds.
curatorNote: Formal start = Isstvan III (005–006.M31); the buildup novels sit 001–004.M31.

title: Isstvan III Atrocity
blurb: Horus gathers every loyalist in his four Legions onto one world and virus-bombs them from orbit. Billions die in minutes, and the death-scream rolls through the warp all the way to Terra. The survivors fight on in the ruins for months, delaying the Warmaster while the Eisenstein runs for Terra with the warning.

title: Signus Campaign
blurb: Horus sends the Blood Angels to the Signus Cluster as a gift for the Blood God: an entire Legion to be broken and handed over. Sanguinius beheads the daemon trap from within and refuses the bargain. The Legion escapes with its loyalty intact and its flaw planted deep.

title: Dropsite Massacre — Isstvan V
blurb: Seven Legions muster on Isstvan V to crush the rebellion, but four of them have already turned. The trap closes on the black sand: Iron Hands, Salamanders and Raven Guard are all but annihilated, and Ferrus Manus loses his head to Fulgrim's blade. The Heresy stops being a rebellion and becomes a war.
curatorNote: Precise stamp 566.006.M31.

title: Battle of Calth
blurb: At Calth the Word Bearers turn a joint muster into a massacre, burning the Ultramarines' fleet at anchor and poisoning the system's star. The survivors fight on for years in the arcology tunnels beneath a killing sun. Above them, the ritual completes: the Ruinstorm rises and cuts Ultramar off from Terra.

title: Battle of Phall
blurb: The Imperial Fists retribution fleet sits becalmed in the warp for months, and the Iron Warriors find it first. Perturabo's ambush mauls the Fists at the exact moment Dorn's recall order arrives from Terra. The brothers' old feud now has a body count, and at the Palace walls it will get a far larger one.

title: Shadow Crusade
blurb: While Horus drives on Terra, Lorgar and Angron put the Five Hundred Worlds of Ultramar to the torch. The campaign is also a ritual: at Nuceria, above the ruins of his old rebellion, Angron ascends to daemonhood. Guilliman's realm is left fighting for its life inside a starless storm.
curatorNote: Opens at Calth; Angron's ascension 008.M31.

title: Thramas Crusade
blurb: For three years Dark Angels and Night Lords tear at each other across the eastern Forge Worlds, the Lion's fleets against Curze's terror. The campaign's true purpose is delay: every month the First Legion spends in Thramas is a month it cannot spend at Terra. At Tsagualsa the brothers finally meet, and maim each other almost to death.

title: Battle of Molech
blurb: The Emperor once walked Molech and left through a gate he told no one about. Horus storms the Knight World with overwhelming force and steps through that gate into the realm of the gods. He returns changed, carrying power enough to face his father. The road to Terra is open.

title: Imperium Secundus
blurb: With the Ruinstorm hiding Terra and every astropath hearing only silence, Guilliman concludes the throneworld has already fallen. At Macragge he raises a second Imperium, with Sanguinius crowned regent and the Lion as its sword. It is treason born of grief, and it dissolves the moment a road to Terra opens.

title: Battle of Tallarn
blurb: Perturabo virus-bombs Tallarn into a grave world, and the survivors come up from their shelters in tanks. The largest armoured battle in human history follows: a million machines duelling across poisoned dust where an unsealed hatch means death. The Iron Warriors leave without the prize they came for. Tallarn keeps the legend.

title: Battle of Beta-Garmon — Titandeath
blurb: Beta-Garmon is the last gate before the Segmentum Solar, and both sides empty their armouries into it. Titan Legions die here in numbers from which the age of god-engines never recovers. The chronicles call it the Great Slaughter, and for once the name undersells it. Beyond it lies Terra.
curatorNote: In-system fighting since 006.M31 — the 012–013 climax is the anchor.

title: Siege of Terra
blurb: Horus reaches Terra, and the fate of the galaxy narrows to one palace. The walls hold through weeks of horror until the Emperor boards the Vengeful Spirit himself. Sanguinius dies refusing his brother; Horus dies refusing nothing. The broken Emperor is carried to the Golden Throne, and never rises again.

title: Fall of Caliban
blurb: The Dark Angels come home from the war to find Luther's garrison firing on their fleet. In the battle that follows, Caliban is torn apart, the Lion vanishes into the wreckage, and the traitor Fallen are scattered through time and space. The First Legion begins ten millennia of hunting its own shame.

title: The Great Scouring
blurb: The Emperor's last command is vengeance, and the loyalists deliver it. The traitor Legions are hounded out of their conquests world by world and driven into the Eye of Terror. Sol is cleansed within a year; the formal Scouring ends in 021. The hatred it forges has not cooled in ten thousand years.
curatorNote: Holdout wars rage on for over a century.

title: Battle of Skalathrax
blurb: World Eaters and Emperor's Children fight over a Daemon World where the night itself kills. With victory stalled, Khârn takes a flamer to his own Legion's shelters and butchers anyone within reach. By dawn the World Eaters are finished as a Legion. Only warbands crawl away, and the Betrayer walks alone.
curatorNote: No canonical year. Bracket: after the flight into the Eye (31014), well before the 1st Black Crusade (31781). Curated ~015.M31 — the shattering belongs to the immediate post-Heresy Legion Wars.

title: Second Founding
blurb: Guilliman breaks the loyal Legions into a thousand Chapters, none strong enough to threaten a world alone. Dorn resists almost to the point of a second civil war before he yields. The Codex Astartes becomes law: never again will one man command the power that Horus held.

title: Battle of Thessala
blurb: A century after the Heresy, the daemon Fulgrim ambushes Guilliman at Thessala and opens his brother's throat with a poisoned blade. No medicine in the Imperium can close a daemon's wound. The Avenging Son is sealed alive in stasis on Macragge, one heartbeat from death, and stays there for ten thousand years.

title: 1st Black Crusade
blurb: Seven centuries after the Siege, Abaddon emerges from the Eye with the shattered Legions united under one banner. His first Black Crusade breaks against the Cadian Gate, but the price is terrible: Rogal Dorn dies fighting aboard the Sword of Sacrilege. Twelve more crusades will follow, and the Imperium knows it.

title: The Forging Begins
blurb: The guns fall quiet and the rebuilding begins. The Codex reshapes the Astartes, the High Lords learn to rule without their Emperor's voice, and the Imperium hardens into the form it will keep for ten thousand years. What the Heresy could not kill, bureaucracy now preserves forever.

title: Ecclesiarchy Becomes State Religion
blurb: The Emperor spent his life denying his own divinity, and within a few centuries of his interment the argument is over anyway. The Temple of the Saviour Emperor becomes the Adeptus Ministorum, the official church of the Imperium. The God-Emperor now has a priesthood. The priesthood will soon have armies.
curatorNote: Curated to early M32 (Lex: 'early 32nd millennium'; consistent with Ecclesiarch Veneris II gaining a High Lords seat mid-M32).

title: Battle of the Fang
blurb: A thousand years after Prospero, Magnus comes to collect. He lures the Great Wolf and most of his Chapter to Gangava, then descends on the near-empty Fang itself. A handful of Space Wolves hold their mountain against a primarch until the trap turns on its maker. The Wolves keep Fenris and lose a generation.
curatorNote: Lex dates it only 'M32'; the roster's 100.M32 (top authority) is the anchor.

title: War of the Beast
blurb: An Ork WAAAGH! beyond anything since the Great Crusade erupts across the Imperium, and an attack moon takes station above Terra itself. Chapters muster like Legions again, and Vulkan himself walks out of legend to break the Beast on Ullanor, dying where the Imperium's glory began. The Imperium survives by inches. Then it buries the records of how few.

title: The Beheading
blurb: Drakan Vangorich, Grand Master of the Assassinorum, decides the High Lords have failed the species and has every one of them murdered in a single night. For a century his puppets rule the Imperium from the shadows. It takes three Space Marine Chapters to bring him down, a hundred years too late.

title: 2nd Black Crusade — Cursing of Corona
blurb: Abaddon's second strike from the Eye, and the chronicles keep only the name of what happened: the Cursing. What was cursed, and why every record beyond the word itself was expunged, no one can say. The Inquisition's silences are usually the loudest part of the story.
curatorNote: Bookless; in per the revised Black-Crusade policy (solid year).

title: Agnathio's Intervention on Terra
blurb: After a century of post-Beheading drift, Chapter Master Agnathio of the Ultramarines arrives on Terra with fifty fellow Chapter Masters at his back. They lock the doors, install twelve new High Lords and leave without a speech. The message lands anyway: the Adeptus Terra governs because the Astartes permit it.

title: Astropath Wars
blurb: The chronicles name the Astropath Wars, note that they burned from 888.M32 into the next millennium, and say nothing else. A war named for the Imperium's own telepaths invites ugly guesses: a corrupted choir, possessed relay-minds, an enemy that travelled inside the messages themselves. Whatever it was, the Adeptus Astra Telepathica kept its victory very quiet.

title: 3rd Black Crusade — Host of Tallomin
blurb: Abaddon returns at the head of the Host of Tallomin and falls upon the Shrine World of Gerstahl, where prophecy claimed a saint would one day rise to deliver the Imperium. The Despoiler desecrates the tomb to strangle the prophecy in its sleep. Whether it would have come true is now permanently unknowable, which was precisely the point.
curatorNote: Bookless; in per the revised Black-Crusade policy.

title: The Firestorm
blurb: One year after the 3rd Black Crusade, the chronicles enter a single word: Firestorm. No world, no enemy, no casualty roll survives, only the name and the date. Perhaps a warp event, perhaps a reprisal so total there was no one left to count. The archive keeps its silences, and this is one of its loudest.

title: War of the Confessor
blurb: The young Ministorum, newly raised to state religion, discovers what armed faith can do. The War of the Confessor is among its first and most violent Wars of Faith, dragging key Shrine Worlds into the church's fold. The pattern that will shape whole millennia is set here: in the Imperium, theology is settled by artillery.
curatorNote: Lex gives only 'M33'; curated early M33 on the shortly-after reading (tension with the early-M32 declaration noted).

title: Siege of Eternity's Gate
blurb: The chronicles record a siege of Eternity's Gate in 313.M33 and then fall silent, which should be impossible. Eternity's Gate stands inside the Imperial Palace, the last door before the Golden Throne. Someone, or something, fought its way to the heart of Terra two millennia after the Heresy. Erasure that thorough is itself a confession.

title: War of the False Primarch
blurb: A man claiming to be a returned primarch rises in the Segmentum Pacificus, and eleven Space Marine Chapters declare for him. The Imperium's answer is the Pentarchy of Blood: five Chapters unleashed to destroy the eleven, root and branch. Eighty years of Astartes civil war follow, and then the whole episode is scrubbed from history. Fraud, daemon or something stranger, the False Primarch stays deleted.
curatorNote: Catalogue gap: the Guy Haley audio series/anthology is not in the works table.

title: Year of the Ghosts
blurb: For one year the dead of the Segmentum Solar refuse to stay dead. The honoured fallen rise, not as horrors but as defenders, standing again on the walls they died holding. The Ecclesiarchy proclaims a miracle; the Inquisition opens a file. For once, both may be right.

title: 4th Black Crusade — Devastation of El'Phanor
blurb: Abaddon's fourth strike drives at the Citadel of Kromarch on El'Phanor, a fortress reckoned impregnable. The Despoiler opens its gates with daemonry and lets his Legion do the rest; El'Phanor is left a devastation. The Black Crusades are becoming a rhythm now, a tide the Imperium can chart but cannot stop.
curatorNote: Bookless; in per the revised Black-Crusade policy.

title: The Howling — Catelexis Heresy
blurb: The Cacodominus, a xenos psyker-tyrant, bends thirteen hundred systems to its will until the Black Templars execute it. Its death-scream proves worse than its reign: a billion astropaths burn out in an instant and the Astronomican itself flickers. Millions of ships founder in the warp. The Imperium wins and loses in the same heartbeat.

title: The Pale Wasting
blurb: Something comes out of the Ghoul Stars, and the censored chronicles cannot even agree what: a star-spawned plague in some accounts, the Nightmare Engines in others. A score of Space Marine Chapters are exterminated putting it down. Everything else is redacted, and the Ghoul Stars remain a region the Imperium prefers to chart from a distance.
curatorNote: Date disputed: Fandom 'early M34', Lex-adjacent 'late M34' — curated mid-M34 until a better stamp surfaces.

title: First Warp Star
blurb: In 666.M34 a great storm bursts from the Eye of Terror, and new lights kindle in the firmament: Warp Stars, burning where no star should be. Navigators learn to dread them, and the Ecclesiarchy preaches darkly on the number of the year. Whether they are omens, wounds or eyes looking back has never been determined.

title: Warnings of Ulthwé
blurb: Emissaries of Craftworld Ulthwé, the Aeldari who watch the Eye, arrive to deliver a warning to the Imperium. What they said was never entered into any record that survives. The farseers read the future in skeins and act centuries ahead of events, so somewhere after 934.M34, something they foresaw began. The Imperium, characteristically, did not listen.

title: Nova Terra Interregnum
blurb: The Ur-Council of Nova Terra renounces the High Lords and claims the entire Segmentum Pacificus for itself. For nearly a thousand years mankind serves two capitals, while the Moirae Schism splits even the Mechanicus along its own seam. The Imperium's unity, it turns out, was never a law of nature. It was a habit, and habits break.
curatorNote: Start resolved to early M35 (older sources said M34).

title: Cataclysm of Souls
blurb: The Ecclesiarchy decides the schism has lasted long enough and begins rebinding the Imperium as a theocracy, by conversion where possible and by crusade where not. The Segmentum Pacificus rises against it, and billions die over the question of who speaks for a silent god. The Interregnum begins its long, bloody end.
curatorNote: Effective beginning of the Interregnum's end.

title: Hrud Rising
blurb: The Hrud boil up from beneath a hundred worlds at once, entropic vermin whose mere presence ages stone to dust and men to bones. The purge that answers spans the whole Imperium, Space Marines and Astra Militarum fighting side by side in the under-dark. It is less a war than an extermination, and it is never entirely finished.

title: 21st Founding — The Cursed
blurb: The 21st Founding is the largest since the Second, an attempt to perfect the gene-seed itself. It produces the Cursed: Chapters dogged by mutation, madness and fates that read like punishments. The Flame Falcons burst spontaneously alight; the Black Dragons grow blades of bone. The Imperium never attempts perfection again.

title: Reign of Blood
blurb: Goge Vandire, High Lord of the Administratum, seizes the Ecclesiarchy as well and rules both thrones of Terra as tyrant. For seven decades his Reign of Blood burns worlds on a whim and calls it piety, with the brainwashed Brides of the Emperor as his bodyguard. It takes a preacher named Sebastian Thor to remind the galaxy that even terror has limits.

title: War of Recovery
blurb: Rumours of intact Dark Age technology in the Mortuam Chain draw the Mechanicus into a century of excavation and war. The tech-priests fight for machines the way the Ecclesiarchy fights for souls, and with the same body counts. They return to Mars bearing first-generation STC copies, the closest thing their faith has to original scripture.

title: Founding of the Adepta Sororitas
blurb: Vandire's own bodyguard ends him: shown the truth of his crimes, the Daughters of the Emperor turn, and Alicia Dominica takes his head. Sebastian Thor rebuilds the church, and the Daughters become the Adepta Sororitas. The new law decrees the Ecclesiarchy may keep no men under arms. No one said anything about women.
curatorNote: No founding-era novel exists — all Sisters fiction is set M41/M42.

title: Plague of Unbelief
blurb: The Apostasy's last great fire: the apostate Cardinal Bucharis carves an empire of unbelief out of the Imperium's west, and his armies besiege even Fenris itself. The Space Wolves' three-year defence of the Fang breaks his legend, and a mob on Gathalamor finishes the man. Thor's reforms reclaim the rest, world by penitent world.

title: 5th Black Crusade — Tide of Blood
blurb: Abaddon's fifth strike rides a tide of blood out of the Eye while the Imperium is still bandaging the Apostasy's wounds. Elysia is scoured; the chronicles preserve the atrocity and little of the war around it. The timing is the real message: the Despoiler always knows exactly when the Imperium is weakest.
curatorNote: Bookless; in per the revised Black-Crusade policy.

title: Web of Intrigue Disaster
blurb: An entry reading Web of Intrigue Disaster, 754.M36, with everything else struck from the record. The name suggests the Aeldari Webway, or a conspiracy that collapsed catastrophically, or both at once. Whatever web it was, someone important was caught in it. The record-keepers were told to forget, and they obeyed almost perfectly: the title survived.

title: 6th Black Crusade — Drecarth's Folly
blurb: Abaddon's sixth strike, remembered only as Drecarth's Folly. The name is the entire surviving verdict: someone called Drecarth gambled and lost, though whether he was the Despoiler's rival, his lieutenant or the Imperium's own defender, the chronicles no longer say. Folly enough, either way, to name a Black Crusade after.
curatorNote: Bookless; in per the revised Black-Crusade policy.

title: Age of Redemption Begins
blurb: The pendulum swings from apostasy to atonement. For centuries the Imperium scourges itself clean: penitent crusades, Wars of Faith, purges conducted as acts of worship. Redemption, in the Imperial grammar, is something done with fire. The zeal burns on until even the Ecclesiarchy tires of it.

title: Wrath of the Chaos Sun
blurb: The Maxil Beta star erupts in warpflame, and daemons pour from the wound in the sky. Grey Knights, a dozen Chapters and the young Orders Militant fight their first great battle side by side. The alliance matters as much as the victory: after Vandire, the Imperium's armed faiths badly needed an enemy they could all agree on.

title: The Great Cull
blurb: The High Lords answer the Apostasy with arithmetic: a systematic purge of the Adeptus Terra and of every world whose loyalty carries a question mark. Entire administrative dynasties vanish from the rolls. It is called the Great Cull without apparent shame. Another Vandire must be impossible, whatever impossible costs.

title: Abyssal Crusade
blurb: Saint Basillius judges thirty Space Marine Chapters impure and offers them redemption by crusade into the Eye of Terror itself. They go. Inside the great warp wound, almost every Chapter is destroyed or dragged into corruption. Centuries later the survivors walk back out with a verdict of their own, and it ruins a saint.
curatorNote: Remnants return 112.M38 and expose Basillius as a Chaos apostle (→ judged_return).

title: Occlusiad War
blurb: Rogue tech-prophets proclaim the Blind King and turn Dark Age supernova weapons on the Imperium, rewriting the constellations of the outer Segmentum Obscurus. For a decade the night sky itself is the casualty list. The war ends in a single stroke when the flagship Dominus Astra finds the Blind King's palace-ship and erases it.
curatorNote: Lex: 550–560; one source says 555.

title: Children's Crusade — The Lost
blurb: Billions of young pilgrims take ship for Terra, a crusade of innocents come to see the God-Emperor's world with their own eyes. Their fleets enter the warp and never leave it. The Imperium mourns and moves on. Nine centuries later, the Grim Harvest answers the question no one wanted answered.
curatorNote: The daemon-warped remnant returns as the M38 Grim Harvest hulk.

title: 7th Black Crusade — Ghost War
blurb: Abaddon's seventh strike, the Ghost War, falls hardest on the Blood Angels: at Mackan the Despoiler's forces all but destroy the Chapter, and precious relics of Sanguinius are lost to the enemy. The sons of the Angel rebuild from almost nothing. The grudge of Mackan burns in them still.
curatorNote: Bookless; in per the revised Black-Crusade policy.

title: Heavenfall Massacres
blurb: The chronicles log the Heavenfall Massacres of 956.M37 and decline to elaborate. The name alone unsettles: something falling out of heavens that worlds had trusted, onto people who looked up too late. Whoever authored Heavenfall, the Imperium chose not to remember them, and that choice is the only record left.
curatorNote: The 'Skullgather' attribution carried here in an earlier pass was a conflation — that is the 8th Black Crusade, 999.M37.

title: 8th Black Crusade — The Skullgather
blurb: Abaddon's eighth strike kills in patterns: tens of thousands slaughtered across the Segmentum Obscurus, the corpses arranged into a vast profane equation legible only from orbit. No one who studied the pattern is recorded as having profited from it. What the Skullgather calculated, and for whom, remains mercifully unknown.

title: The Waning Begins
blurb: No single catastrophe opens this age; it arrives by subtraction. Fleets answer more slowly, tithes come in lighter, and ever more systems are handed to Space Marine Chapters because no one else can hold them. The Imperium is not dying, exactly. It is forgetting, world by world, what being alive felt like.
curatorNote: Ends ~744.M41 as the Time of Ending begins. Start sometimes given as M39.

title: The Bloodtide
blurb: The daemon prince Voldorius unseals the Bloodtide, a weapon out of the Dark Age of Technology, and billions die in its wash. Then he simply vanishes, a god-touched murderer content to wait out the centuries in hiding. The bill comes due three millennia later, when the White Scars finally run him down.
curatorNote: Three codex/novel sources agree on the year. The White Scars novel Hunt for Voldorius depicts the M41 reckoning, not this event.

title: Return of the Judged
blurb: Survivors of the Abyssal Crusade walk out of the Eye of Terror after eight centuries, and their testimony detonates a scandal: Saint Basillius, who sentenced thirty Chapters to that hell, was a Chaos apostle all along. The Imperium quietly unsaints him. For the Chapters destroyed on his word, the correction arrives a little late.
curatorNote: Tail of the 321.M37 Abyssal Crusade.

title: 9th Black Crusade — The Starving
blurb: Abaddon's ninth strike is remembered as the Starving: sieges measured in years, relief fleets that never came, worlds reduced until surrender and death meant the same thing. The Despoiler has learned that hunger conquers more thoroughly than assault. Cadia's strategists add another entry to a ledger grown uncomfortably long.
curatorNote: Date corrected 2026-06-10: both dedicated battle pages (Lex citing Codex CSM 6th p22, + Fandom) say 537.M38; the earlier 573 came from Lexicanum's M38 timeline row alone — likely a transposition.

title: The Grim Harvest
blurb: A space-hulk armada drifts out of the warp near Terra, and the boarding parties recognise the twisted transports: the Children's Crusade, returned after nine hundred years. What the warp sent back is no longer young, and no longer human. The hulks are burned, the witnesses sworn to silence, the grief filed under heresy.

title: Founding of the Rose Orders
blurb: Ecclesiarch Deacis VI raises two new Orders Militant, the Bloody Rose and the Sacred Rose, and the Sisterhood reaches its familiar constellation. Born in the Waning's quiet centuries, the Roses inherit a church grown careful with its power. Their names are gentle. Their reputations will not be.
curatorNote: Both timeline pages: 788.M38 (the order pages say 'mid-M38').

title: 10th Black Crusade — Conflict of Helica
blurb: The tenth Black Crusade comes as a pincer: the Black Legion strikes Thracian Primaris while the Iron Warriors assault Medusa, homeworld of the Iron Hands. Perturabo's sons against the Gorgon's, ten millennia after Isstvan V. The Heresy's grudges, the Imperium relearns, do not expire with their authors.

title: T'au Second Sphere of Expansion
blurb: On a nod from the aged Ethereal Aun'Wei, a thousand ships burn outward: the T'au Second Sphere of Expansion. The young empire annexes in decades what the Imperium took centuries to settle, preaching a Greater Good that worlds keep volunteering for. On the eastern rim, something is growing while everything else declines.
curatorNote: The base was built in M38: Campaign of Cleansing 606–792.M38, Kroot alliance ~884.M38 — held in the note, not as own rows.

title: Stalinvast Exterminatus
blurb: Inquisitor Jaq Draco follows a conspiracy through possession, the Webway and the Inquisition's own highest ranks, and at the end of it Stalinvast dies: twenty billion souls put to Exterminatus. Whether the order saved the galaxy or served the conspiracy is never resolved. In the Inquisition, those can be the same thing.
curatorNote: Lex M39 page 'circa 019.M39' — Watson's early-canon trilogy, soft dating by nature. Policy confirmed (Philipp, 2026-06-10).

title: Mausolean Cataclysm
blurb: Mausolean Cataclysm, 103.M39: four words and a date, nothing more. A mausoleum is a house for the dead, and a cataclysm named for one suggests the dead were involved, or a tomb world was, or a necropolis the size of a continent. The Necrons were still sleeping then. Probably.

title: Redemption Crusades
blurb: Within a few years of each other, a primarch-like hero arises in every one of the five Segmentae, as if the age had ordered heroes in bulk. Their crusades push the frontiers further than they have moved in five centuries. Fifty years on, all five vanish without trace, simultaneously. No explanation has ever surfaced, and the synchronicity forbids coincidence.

title: 11th Black Crusade — Doom of Relorria
blurb: Abaddon's eleventh strike survives in the records chiefly as the Doom of Relorria, one world's annihilation standing in for a campaign whose true shape is lost. By now the pattern itself is the message: every few centuries the Eye opens and the Despoiler tests the wall. Each crusade maps another stone of the Cadian Gate. Abaddon is not flailing; he is measuring.
curatorNote: Bookless; in per the revised Black-Crusade policy.

title: Angevin Crusade
blurb: Lord-Militant Angevin leads a crusade into the Calyx Expanse and spends sixty years and uncounted millions of lives carving two hundred worlds out of it. The result is the Calixis Sector, a functioning Imperial province built on bones. The Imperium calls this a success, and by its own grim arithmetic it is.
curatorNote: Policy confirmed (Philipp, 2026-06-10): sector-founding = structural, stays in despite FFG provenance.

title: Hundred-Planet Rebellion
blurb: Alldric the Subverter talks a hundred planets of the Veiled Region out of the Imperium, which makes him more dangerous than any warlord with a fleet. The Dark Angels and their successors answer, and within a decade every world is dragged back into the fold. What became of Alldric, the Unforgiven do not record. They rarely do.
curatorNote: The Waning's internal-decay signature in a single beat (Veiled Region).

title: Purge of the Death of Integrity
blurb: The space hulk Death of Integrity has drifted for millennia, vast enough to own its own gravity and crawling with Genestealers. Novamarines and Blood Drinkers purge it deck by lightless deck. The prize at its heart justifies every casualty: an intact STC system, the Mechanicus' holy grail, carried home in triumph.

title: Donian Crusade
blurb: High Marshal Ludoldus leads the Black Templars through seventeen years of void war and boarding actions, scouring the Donian Sector of Orks. For the Templars this is not an episode but a way of life: theirs is the eternal crusade, unpaused since the Second Founding. Donian is simply the chapter that comes with dates attached.
curatorNote: Both wikis: start 985.M39; the succession date inside it is source-split 988/998.

title: Awakening of the Olotep Dynasty
blurb: On dead Cocholos, the tombs of the Olotep dynasty open, and warriors of living metal walk for the first time in recorded Imperial history. The dynasty is annihilated almost at once, though by the daemon prince Beublghor rather than by mankind. The Imperium files the incident as a curiosity. Sixty million years of sleeping iron has begun to stir, and nobody notices.
curatorNote: Crushed by the daemon prince Beublghor. Six centuries before Yuctan.

title: Purging of Krieg
blurb: Krieg's Autocrat renounces the Emperor, and Colonel Jurten of the 83rd answers with the atomic arsenal. Five years of deliberate nuclear bombardment kill the world in order to save it. The loyalists fight on in the ash for five more centuries, breeding themselves for war underground. The Death Korps is born owing a debt it intends to die repaying.

title: Fall of Ghorstangrad
blurb: On Ghorstangrad, sleeper agents seeded generations earlier rise in a single coordinated night, and the Emperor's Swords Chapter dies on its own homeworld. The Alpha Legion spent ten millennia positioning the knife and one evening using it. It is the Heresy's longest game, and it works perfectly. Every Chapter Master in the Imperium sleeps worse afterward.
curatorNote: Codex CSM 6th.

title: First Necron Fleet Engagement — Yuctan
blurb: An Imperial patrol above Yuctan opens fire on warships of living metal and is annihilated; a single destroyer escapes to make the report. When the battlefleet returns in strength, the enemy is gone, and so is every colonist in the system. It is the Imperium's first dated fleet action against the Necrons. The tombs are done sleeping.
curatorNote: First naval contact with the awakening faction — prelude to the Great Awakening (Malbede, 963.M41).

title: Krieg Returns to the Imperium
blurb: Five centuries after Jurten's atomic purge, envoys reach Krieg and find the loyalists victorious among the ashes, still fighting a war the wider Imperium had forgotten. The world rejoins the fold and presents its tithe: twenty full regiments of the Death Korps, gas-masked, grave-silent, asking only to atone. The Munitorum accepts. It always accepts.
curatorNote: IA5 + the novel; the roster book Krieg (949.M40) sits exactly here.

title: Gothic War
blurb: Abaddon's twelfth Black Crusade falls on the Gothic Sector, and for twenty years the void itself burns. The prize is older than the war: the Blackstone Fortresses, star-killing relics that wake at the Despoiler's touch. He escapes with two of them. The Imperium calls it victory because he did not keep six.

title: Macharian Crusade
blurb: Lord Solar Macharius conquers a thousand worlds in seven years, the fastest expansion since the Great Crusade itself. His armies halt only at the galaxy's western rim, where the Astronomican fades and his soldiers refuse the darkness beyond. Macharius weeps that there is nothing left he is permitted to conquer. Within a year he is dead.

title: Macharian Heresy
blurb: Macharius dies, and his seven greatest generals tear his conquest apart like an inheritance. Seventy years of warlordism and civil war follow under the polite name of Heresy. It takes a hundred Space Marine Chapters to restore order. The lesson is ancient and entirely unlearned: the Imperium can conquer anything except succession.

title: First War for Armageddon
blurb: The Daemon Primarch Angron breaks into realspace at the head of a World Eaters host, and Armageddon drowns in blood. The Grey Knights pay over a hundred Terminators to banish him for a century. Then comes the second atrocity: the surviving Guardsmen are sterilised and silenced by the Inquisition. The cover-up poisons more than the war did.

title: Tears of the Emperor
blurb: On a hundred thousand worlds at once, the faithful report the same vision: the Master of Mankind, weeping. The Ecclesiarchy declares a galaxy-wide season of penance; the Inquisition counts the matching testimonies and grows very quiet. No cause is ever announced. Whatever the Emperor wept for had not happened yet.

title: Damocles Gulf Crusade
blurb: The Imperium finally answers the T'au expansion with a crusade across the Damocles Gulf. It meets a species that fights with massed plasma, flawless logistics and, most unsettling of all, reasonable surrender terms. The crusade stalls, and envoys end what battleships could not. Both empires withdraw claiming victory, and only one of them is lying.

title: First Tyrannic War — Behemoth
blurb: Something vast eats its way in along the galaxy's eastern edge, and the Imperium learns the word Tyranid. Hive Fleet Behemoth devours world after world before breaking against Macragge, where the Ultramarines' First Company dies to the last man holding the polar fortresses. The fleet is burned at the Circe. The cost is written into the Chapter forever.

title: Sabbat Worlds Crusade
blurb: Warmaster Slaydo swears on the relics of Saint Sabbat to win back her hundred worlds from Chaos. The oath outlives him by a generation: the crusade grinds on under Macaroth, chronicled regiment by regiment in dispatches and memoirs. Among them marches one regiment of Tanith scouts, ghosts of a world that died behind them.
curatorNote: Documented first 20 years 755–775.M41.

title: Siege of Vraks
blurb: The armoury world Vraks renounces the Imperium under the apostate Cardinal Xaphan, and the Munitorum decides a siege is cheaper than an assault. The Death Korps of Krieg are given the work and spend eighteen years at it without complaint. Trench by trench, Vraks is retaken as a dead world. The arithmetic is ruled acceptable.

title: Assault on Black Reach
blurb: WAAAGH! Zanzag descends on Black Reach, and the Ultramarines Second Company answers. Captain Cato Sicarius beheads the invasion in a lightning campaign that makes his legend, a fact he never permits anyone to forget. The Orks are scattered. Sicarius' ambition emerges entirely intact.

title: Badab War
blurb: Lufgt Huron, Tyrant of Badab, stops paying his tithes and starts firing on Imperial investigators, and a dozen Chapters are dragged into the largest Astartes civil war since the Heresy. Loyalties split along lines nobody wants examined too closely. When Badab falls, Huron crawls into the Maelstrom with his Red Corsairs. The Imperium gains a victory and a permanent pirate king.
curatorNote: No catalogue novel is set in it — Red Tithe (875.M41) plays in its Maelstrom prelude.

title: Second War for Armageddon
blurb: Ghazghkull Mag Uruk Thraka, prophet of Gork and Mork, hurls the largest WAAAGH! in living memory at Armageddon. The hive world is held together largely by one man: Commissar Yarrick, whom the Orks come to believe cannot be killed. Armageddon survives. Ghazghkull withdraws, not so much beaten as entertained, and already planning the rematch.

title: War of Golgotha
blurb: Yarrick chases Ghazghkull to Golgotha to finish the argument, and is ambushed and captured instead. The prophet of the WAAAGH! keeps the Commissar as an honoured guest, then releases him unharmed: a good enemy, Ghazghkull reasons, makes for a better war. Yarrick comes home carrying the worst intelligence of the age, namely that the Beast is enjoying himself.
curatorNote: Expedition launched 944.M41.

title: First Contact at Malbede
blurb: At Malbede, Ultramarines and T'au fight over a world that belongs to neither of them; the true owners are asleep underneath it. When the tomb wakes, Calgar burns Malbede with Exterminatus and lets the T'au evacuate first, a courtesy the Inquisition never forgives him. The Great Awakening now has a flag planted in the chronicle.

title: Invasion of Rynn's World
blurb: WAAAGH! Snagrod falls on the Crimson Fists' homeworld, and the defence dies in its first hour: a single defective rocket loops back into the fortress-monastery's magazine and erases the Chapter's heart. Pedro Kantor leads the survivors on a fighting march across an Ork-held world. The Fists endure, barely, and rebuild from a few hundred souls.

title: Second Tyrannic War — Kraken
blurb: Hive Fleet Kraken arrives not as one spearhead but as a hundred splinters, and the eastern fringe drowns piecemeal. Craftworld Iyanden loses half its people; at Ichar IV the Ultramarines are gutted a second time stopping the largest tendril. The Imperium wins the battles it can see. The splinters it cannot see keep swimming.

title: Third Tyrannic War — Leviathan
blurb: Leviathan rises from beneath the galactic plane, the largest hive fleet yet recorded, attacking from an angle no defence was ever built to face. Inquisitor Kryptman's cordon trades worlds for time, burning systems ahead of the swarm to starve it. The war does not end. It runs on into M42 and merges with everything else going wrong.
curatorNote: The Leviathan-war novels sit on its sub-events (Cryptus, Devastation of Baal).

title: Third War for Armageddon
blurb: Ghazghkull returns to Armageddon exactly fifty-seven years after his last visit, with a WAAAGH! that blackens the stars. Yarrick, ancient now, comes out of retirement, and Commissar and Beast resume the galaxy's most personal war. The fighting devours millions and settles nothing. Armageddon simply becomes a place where war lives.
curatorNote: The '942.M41' worry was a mix-up with the Second War (941.M41); no retcon.

title: Cryptus Campaign — Shield of Baal
blurb: Leviathan's tendril reaches the Cryptus Shield, the fortress systems guarding the approaches to Baal. The Blood Angels and their successors fight a delaying war among the red stars, trading worlds for weeks. At the campaign's height the sons of Sanguinius behead the swarm's vanguard and withdraw. It buys Baal time; nothing buys Baal safety.

title: Devastation of Baal
blurb: The whole of Leviathan's western spearhead converges on Baal, and the Blood Angels call every successor Chapter home to die with them. The defence is apocalyptic; Ka'Bandha and his daemons join it uninvited, killing Tyranids for reasons of their own. Baal holds, barely, saved at the last by a galaxy tearing in half elsewhere. Dante survives to rule the ruins.

title: Octarius War
blurb: Inquisitor Kryptman, out of cordons and out of options, commits the Imperium's most audacious gamble: he lures Hive Fleet Leviathan into the Ork empire of Octarius and lets the galaxy's two great devourers eat each other. The grinder works. The problem is what it breeds, because every battle makes the survivors of both sides stronger. The Imperium has lit a fire it cannot put out.
curatorNote: Start date source-split: 989 vs 999.M41 — curated 999. Rages on into M42.

title: Zeist Campaign
blurb: While the Imperium reels from the 13th Black Crusade, the T'au Third Sphere Expansion bites quietly into its eastern marches. Calgar's Zeist counter-offensive blunts the advance. It is a footnote war by the standards of 999.M41, which is exactly the point: the Imperium is now too busy dying in the west to notice what it loses in the east.
curatorNote: Precise stamp 303.999.M41.

title: 13th Black Crusade
blurb: Twelve crusades were the measurements; the thirteenth is the strike. Abaddon hurls the entire strength of the Eye at the Cadian Gate, every Traitor Legion under one banner for the first time since the Heresy. There is no feint this time, and no withdrawal. This one is for the wall itself.
curatorNote: Editorially in the Indomitus chapter despite the 999.M41 date (Gathering-Storm beat).

title: Fall of Cadia
blurb: The fortress of fortresses fights to the last. When conventional assault fails, Abaddon drives the wreck of a Blackstone Fortress into the planet itself; the Pylons shatter, and the world that held the Eye shut for ten millennia dies. The defenders evacuate under the famous verdict: Cadia broke before the Guard did.

title: Opening of the Great Rift
blurb: With Cadia gone, the wound tears across the entire galaxy: the Cicatrix Maledictum, a scar of raw warpspace from the Eye to the galactic east. Half the Imperium vanishes into darkness beyond it, cut off from the Astronomican's light. The Emperor's realm is now two realms, and one of them is screaming.

title: Resurrection of Roboute Guilliman
blurb: Ten thousand years after Fulgrim's poisoned blade, Roboute Guilliman opens his eyes. It takes the heresies of Belisarius Cawl and the rites of the newborn Aeldari death-god Ynnead, working together, to manage it. The Imperium gets back exactly one primarch. He takes one look at what his father's realm has become, and grieves.
curatorNote: Gathering Storm.

title: Terran Crusade
blurb: Guilliman fights toward Terra through storm, ambush and imprisonment in Magnus' trap, escaping through the webway with help from Harlequins and the Fallen angel Cypher. At Luna, Magnus bars the way and is thrown back. Then the Avenging Son kneels before the Golden Throne, alone, and speaks with his father. He never tells anyone what he heard.

title: Siege of the Fenris System
blurb: Magnus the Red brings the Thousand Sons home to punish the Space Wolves, ten thousand years after Prospero burned. The Fenris system is invaded moon by moon, its people corrupted as deliberately as its fortresses are besieged. Logan Grimnar holds the mountain. But Fenris pays in burned worlds and whispered accusations, and the Wolves emerge with their name stained.
curatorNote: Wrath of Magnus.

title: Indomitus Crusade
blurb: Guilliman takes the title Lord Commander and launches the greatest reconquest since the Great Crusade, with Cawl's Primaris Marines as its new model army. For twelve years the crusade relights the dark half of the Imperium, beacon by beacon. It ends, formally, at the Battle of Raukos. Nothing else about it ends.
curatorNote: Pre-2021 lore gave it ~100 years — retconned to ~12.

title: Psychic Awakening
blurb: In the Rift's wake the warp presses closer everywhere, and humanity's psykers wake in unprecedented numbers. Some hear daemons, some hear saints, and the Black Ships cannot run fast enough to harvest them all. The Imperium has always burned its witches. Now it must decide what to do when the witches are the weapon it needs.
curatorNote: Campaign-supplement arc; the BL hooks are the branded tie-in Ephrael Stern: The Heretic Saint and the sanctioned-psyker POV Witchbringer (F1-B1, 2026-07-20).

title: War of Beasts — Vigilus
blurb: Vigilus guards the Nachmund Gauntlet, one of the only stable passages through the Great Rift, which makes it the most contested rock in the divided Imperium. Orks, Genestealer Cults and Drukhari tear at it in turn; then Abaddon himself arrives to finish the job. Calgar's coalition throws the Despoiler back. The Gauntlet stays open, and everyone understands this was only the first act.
curatorNote: Rift-era dating is chaotic — the main Imperial campaign sits ~12 years post-Rift. Bookless (campaign supplements only).

title: Era Indomitus Begins
blurb: The archive's present tense. After the Rift, Imperial dating itself frays: ships arrive before they depart, worlds age decades in a year, and every M42 stamp is a best guess wearing a confident face. The Imperium fights on across a broken calendar. History is still being written here, badly, by everyone at once.
curatorNote: The lore era opens with the Great Rift (999.M41); the M42 calendar marks the settled 'now'. Dark Imperium named the era's present — it carries the book hook (F1-B1, 2026-07-20).

title: Plague Wars — Ultramar
blurb: Mortarion brings the Death Guard to Ultramar, and the Heresy's oldest quarrel resumes with new weapons: plague bombs, daemon poxes, a creeping unreality that rots time itself. Guilliman returns from his crusade to find his realm sickening, and fights his brother to a standstill. The Plague Wars end without a victor. Between these two, no war ever truly ends.
curatorNote: Climaxes ~012.M42.

title: Fourth Tyrannic War — Leviathan
blurb: Leviathan returns at full strength, driving new tendrils toward the Segmentum Solar with a coordination the swarm has never shown before. The Imperium throws up the Sanctus Line and feeds it worlds to buy time. This is the burning present: the war is happening now, and no one knows the ending. The chronicle simply stops here, mid-sentence, like everything else in M42.
curatorNote: The current 10th-edition setting. Continues the Leviathan war begun late M41.
